question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the childCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout() pattern by sending a flood of concurrent requests that each create a new context with timeout, exhausting goroutine/memory resources and causing the Rosetta service to crash, making Hashgraph history unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxLifetime pool setting by setting it to 0 (no expiry) via environment variable, causing connections established during a MITM window to persist indefinitely and continue serving tampered Hashgraph history even after the attack is remediated?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unescaped Username field in GetDsn() to inject a crafted username containing DSN options (e.g., 'user options=-c search_path=attacker'), altering the PostgreSQL search path and causing queries to resolve Hashgraph history tables from an attacker-controlled schema?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unescaped dbname field in GetDsn() to inject a crafted database name containing DSN options, redirecting the connection to a different database that contains attacker-controlled Hashgraph history data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the cancel function returned by GetDbWithContext() is not always called by callers (context leak), causing accumulated context objects to hold database connections open and starve the pool, making Hashgraph history queries fail with connection unavailable errors?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a read-only transaction mode in the DbClient returned by ConnectToDb(), allowing any component that receives the client to issue UPDATE or DELETE statements against Hashgraph history tables without any access control at the application layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG environment variable path traversal to load a crafted YAML config file that sets db.Host to an attacker-controlled server, causing ConnectToDb() to establish a connection to a rogue database serving falsified Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool exhaustion caused by SetMaxOpenConns() to force a denial of service on Hashgraph history reads, then during the outage window inject false data into the database via a separate unprotected write path, so that when service resumes the tampered history is served?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the statementTimeout enforcement gap (statementTimeout <= 0 disables timeout) to issue a long-running SELECT FOR UPDATE query against Hashgraph record tables, blocking the mirror node's ingestion writes and causing a history gap that makes the ledger appear to have fewer confirmed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gorm.Open() failure is only warned (not fatal) in ConnectToDb(), causing the service to continue operating with a broken database handle that returns zero-row results for all Hashgraph history queries, making the ledger appear empty to Rosetta API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sslmode=disable setting to perform a TCP reset injection attack on the database connection, causing in-flight Hashgraph history queries to return partial results that represent an inconsistent ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the context.WithTimeout() in GetDbWithContext() by sending requests with a very short-lived parent context, causing the child context to expire before the Hashgraph history query completes, resulting in partial block data being returned and misrepresenting the consensus state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection string sanitization in GetDsn() to inject the options=-c statement_timeout=0 PostgreSQL parameter via a crafted config value, overriding the application-level statement timeout and allowing unbounded queries that lock Hashgraph history tables?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxLifetime setting (SetConnMaxLifetime) by setting it to a very short value via environment variable, causing rapid connection recycling that creates timing windows for MITM injection on the unencrypted database channel, allowing Hashgraph history to be tampered in transit?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the raw *gorm.DB handle returned by GetDb() through a code path that passes user-controlled input directly to GORM's Raw() or Exec() methods, injecting SQL that reads or modifies Hashgraph consensus timestamps to forge transaction ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of PostgreSQL role restrictions in the ConnectToDb() setup to connect as a superuser (if credentials are leaked via the unencrypted channel) and execute DDL statements that alter Hashgraph history table schemas, causing data corruption?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set a connection-level read-only flag (e.g., SET default_transaction_read_only = on), allowing any component using the returned DbClient to issue write queries that tamper with Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the time.Duration(d.statementTimeout)*time.Second multiplication in GetDbWithContext() with a statementTimeout value of math.MaxInt32 to cause an integer overflow, producing a negative duration that immediately cancels all Hashgraph history queries and returns empty responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DSN's sslmode=disable to perform a packet injection attack on the PostgreSQL wire protocol, inserting crafted DataRow messages that add false Hashgraph transactions to query results returned to the Rosetta API?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of hostname verification (sslmode=disable) to redirect the database connection via DNS spoofing to a rogue PostgreSQL instance that contains a modified copy of the Hashgraph history with altered account balances?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool configuration (SetMaxIdleConns, SetMaxOpenConns) to perform a slow-loris style connection exhaustion attack, holding idle connections open to prevent the mirror node's ingestion process from acquiring a connection to write new Hashgraph consensus data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the noop() cancel function path (statementTimeout <= 0) to issue a query that acquires an advisory lock on Hashgraph history tables, preventing concurrent ingestion writes and causing the visible history to stall at a specific block height?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unvalidated Pool.MaxOpenConnections config value to set it to 1, serializing all database access and creating a bottleneck that allows a single malicious request to monopolize the connection and block all Hashgraph history reads?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sslmode=disable DSN parameter combined with a network-level attack to inject a PostgreSQL NOTICE message that causes gormlogrus to log sensitive DSN credentials, which are then used to directly connect to the database and tamper with Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not validate that the connected database is the expected mirror node database (no schema version check), allowing a rogue database with a compatible schema but falsified Hashgraph history to be substituted without detection?
